title: NEET UG 2026 Counselling: A Step-by-Step Registration Guide
summary: Confused about MCC registration? Here is a complete walkthrough of the online counselling registration, security fee payments, and verification steps.
content:
Counselling registration is the most critical phase after the NEET UG results. Candidates must register on the MCC portal for All India Quota (AIQ) seats. Steps include:
1. Personal details validation.
2. Non-refundable fee & security deposit payment.
3. Documents upload (keep scorecard, admit card, and category certificate ready).
Verify all inputs as editing is not permitted once submitted.
author: Dr. Amit Sharma (Medical Counsellor)

title: Top 10 Documents Required for NEET UG Verification
summary: Avoid last-minute rejection! Ensure you have these ten essential certificates and identity cards ready for physical verification during admissions.
content:
Keep multiple attested copies of the following documents ready before round allotments:
- NEET UG Admit Card & Scorecard
- Class 10 & 12 Pass Certificate & Marks Sheets
- Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, Passport, or PAN Card)
- Category Certificate (if applicable, SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwD)
- Character Certificate from the school last attended
- Provisional Allotment Letter issued by MCC
author: RankZen Editorial Board

title: How to Strategize Choice Filling to Secure Your Dream Seat
summary: Smart choice entry can save you from losing a government college seat. Learn the lock-and-submit priority order formulas used by top rankers.
content:
Choice entry determines your allotment order. Always place premium government institutions at the top even if your rank is slightly lower. Group your choices as:
1. Aspiration choices (top tier colleges you might get in mock round).
2. Realistic choices (colleges matching your rank trend).
3. Safety choices (assured seat allocation colleges).
Never leave choices unlocked; lock them before the official deadline.
author: Prof. S. K. Roy (Counselling Analyst)

title: Understanding NEET UG Quotas: AIQ vs. State Quota Seats
summary: Learn the eligibility and reservation distributions between All India Quota (15%) and State Quota (85%) medical admissions.
content:
Undergraduate medical admissions in India fall under two primary allocations:
- **15% All India Quota (AIQ):** Managed by MCC, open to all candidates nationwide without domicile restrictions.
- **85% State Quota:** Conducted by state authorities, strictly reserved for candidates matching state-defined domicile rules.
Knowing your eligibility percentages helps maximize your admissions strategy.
author: Dr. Amit Sharma (Medical Counsellor)
